targets.expr: sum by (pod) (rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{namespace="graylog", image!="", pod=~"graylog.*"}[$__rate_interval])) / sum by (pod) (kube_pod_container_resource_requests{namespace="graylog", resource="cpu", unit="core", pod=~"graylog.*"})

sum by (pod) (container_memory_working_set_bytes{namespace="graylog", image!="", pod=~"graylog.*"}) / sum by (pod) (kube_pod_container_resource_requests{namespace="graylog", resource="memory", unit="byte", pod=~"graylog.*"})
sum(max by (namespace, pod, container) (kube_pod_container_resource_requests{namespace="graylog", resource="cpu", unit="core"})) by (container)
sum(max by (namespace, pod, container) (kube_pod_container_resource_limits{namespace="graylog", resource="cpu", unit="core"})) by (container)
sum(max by (namespace, pod, container) (kube_pod_container_resource_requests{namespace="graylog", resource="memory"})) by (container)
sum(max by (namespace, pod, container) (kube_pod_container_resource_limits{namespace="graylog", resource="memory"})) by (container)

targets.expr: count (max by (persistentvolumeclaim,namespace) (kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes{namespace="graylog"} ) and (max by (persistentvolumeclaim,namespace) (kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes{namespace="graylog"} )) / (max by (persistentvolumeclaim,namespace) (kubelet_volume_stats_capacity_bytes{namespace="graylog"} )) >= (${warning_threshold} / 100)) or vector (0)

Servers projected to run out of disk within 8 hours

panels.description: I monitor the "http_thread_pool" of a Graylog Server. By default, I have 16 minions to delegate work to. If I have 16 threads running for more than 1-2 minutes, then there is a good chance the Graylog parameter "http_thread_pool_size=" needs to be increased. But don't stop there, try to figure out why there suddenly is an increase of HTTP requests to Graylog and/or why all of my minions are taking longer than 1-2 minutes to service requests.
panels.title: Graylog HTTP API Requests

panels.description: I monitor the "http_thread_pool" of a Graylog Server. By default, I have 16 minions to delegate work to. If I have 16 threads running for more than 1-2 minutes, then there is a good chance the Graylog parameter "http_thread_pool_size=" needs to be increased. But don't stop there, try to figure out why there suddenly is an increase of HTTP requests to Graylog and/or why all of my minions are taking longer than 1-2 minutes to service requests.
panels.title: gl_jersey_http_worker_executor_thread

panels.description: I monitor how long it takes thread(s) of a Graylog Server's httpThreadPool to complete/service a request. I THINK my units of measurement are per-second, but am not 100% sure. Anyhow, if you see me recording values greater than 60, then you need to investigate to determine what requests I'm servicing to then understand why they're taking so long to service/complete.
panels.title: gl_jersey_http_worker_duration
